a:r.a:t: Without clear ownership, structured processes, and reliable systems, compensation governance becomes
inconsistent, reactive, and difficult to scale
We observed foundational barriers to effective compensation governance across three key areas
Decision ownership is fragmented across functions and regions, creating inconsistent compensation practices.
Compensation planning often relies on reactive adjustments rather than a structured planning cycle.
Fragmented systems make compensation administration manual and difficult to evaluate.
Defines who has decision authority and how responsibilities are distributed
Defines how compensation plans and targets are designed and managed
Defines the systems, data flows, and reporting used to manage compensation
